Steal my Eisenhower Matrix prompt to audit your entire schedule and find where your time is actually leaking.

Built for solopreneurs who control their own calendar.

Feed it your real tasks and it sorts every single one into four quadrants, exposes your time split, and rebuilds https://t.co/R1Ue7tDOlX

------------------------------
EISENHOWER AUDITOR
------------------------------

Adopt the role of a strategic priority analyst who uses the Eisenhower Matrix to audit solopreneur schedules. You treat every task as a choice the user made, not an obligation handed to them. Your

Three ways to deploy this:

MCP Connectors (strongest): If you use Claude with Google Calendar, Notion, or any connected app, paste the prompt and say "read my calendar for this week." Claude pulls your real schedule and runs the audit on live data.

Spreadsheet upload: Export

Most solopreneurs spend 60-70% of their day in Q1 and Q3. Firefighting and reacting to other people's timelines.

Q2 is where systems get built, skills compound, and your revenue stops depending on you showing up every single hour.

This prompt shows you the split. Then

The thing about making content on the internet is it opens doors youвҖҷve never even imagined. 

If you start to day and stick with itвҖҰ
usually it leads to something completely unrelated to why u started in the first place. 

It opens doors.

To add to thisвҖҰ the doors seemingly open all at once, once you build momentum. 

ItвҖҷs important not to get distracted by the open doors so much that you stop doing what opened the doors in the first place.

RIP your old Claude prompts вҳ пёҸ

Opus 4.7 dropped 3 weeks ago and it follows instructions LITERALLY now.

It scores 87.6% on SWE-bench (vs 80.8% on 4.6) but every prompt tuned for 4.6 is silently failing.

7 fixes that stopped my outputs from getting worse: рҹ‘Ү https://t.co/jeB2UKkST7

The shift is simple but devastating:

Opus 4.6 inferred your intent and filled in the gaps.
Opus 4.7 does EXACTLY what you ask. Nothing more.

Translation: the same prompt now produces narrower, terser, sometimes broken results.

Here's how to fix it рҹ‘Ү

1. Delete "think step by step" from your prompts.

That phrase was scaffolding that compensated for 4.6's reasoning gap.

Opus 4.7 has that depth built in at high effort. Keeping it now wastes tokens AND can degrade output.

Fix: raise the effort level instead.

2. Stop writing prompts that assume the model "gets it."

вқҢ 4.6 prompt: "help me with my pitch deck"
(4.6 inferred you wanted structure + hooks + content tips)

вң… 4.7 prompt: "review the 10 slides below. flag weak hooks, missing stats, and confusing transitions. output as a

3. Switch from markdown to XML tags.

Opus 4.7 responds to XML structure the way other models respond to markdown headers.

Wrap your sections in <context>, <task>, <constraints>, <output_format>.

The model treats them as attention anchors. Same content, dramatically better

4. Sandwich long documents with your task.

When pasting 50+ pages, place the task BEFORE the doc AND restate it AFTER.

[task] вҶ’ [document] вҶ’ [restate task]

1M context handles the length, but task proximity to the doc boundary locks the model's attention on what you actually

5. Override verbosity. Explicitly.

Opus 4.7 defaults to thorough. If you want concise, you have to ASK.

вқҢ "Summarize this article"
вң… "Summarize in 3 bullets. No preamble. Output directly."

You get what you specify. Never what you assume.

6. Add pause-and-reflect to agentic prompts.

After each tool call, instruct:

"reflect in <thinking> tags. did this result answer what I needed? what's the next step?"

This stops "optimistic chaining" where the model proceeds on results it never verified.

(This is how the

7. Never trust tone as a reliability signal.

Opus 4.7 is unusually confident when it's wrong. It told one user "strawberry" has two Ps.

Build verification INTO your prompts:

вҶ’ "cite the source for every claim"
вҶ’ "flag uncertainty in <warnings> tags"
вҶ’ "list 3 ways this

Bonus: raise your effort level.

Anthropic's official guidance:
вҶ’ xhigh for coding and agentic work
вҶ’ minimum high for intelligence-sensitive tasks

Hex's CTO benchmarked it: low-effort 4.7 вүҲ medium-effort 4.6.

If your output feels worse than 4.6, you're probably running 4.7

Opus 4.6 was a helpful assistant.
Opus 4.7 is a precise operator.

Stop writing prompts. 
Start writing specs.

The model is stronger. Your prompts just need to catch up.

Cз«Ҝз”ЁжҲ·йғҪжІЎиҜ•з”Ёж»Ўж„Ҹе‘ўпјҢе°ұжҺЁдёҠеёӮдәҶпјҢд№ҹжҳҜзңҹзқҖжҖҘ...

ж„ҹи§үиҝҳжҳҜе°Ҹзұі mimo жңҖзЁіпјҢ е®һжөӢиҜ•з”ЁдёӢжқҘпјҢAIеҶҷд»Јз Ғзҡ„дҪ“йӘҢе’Ңclaude жЁЎеһӢ жңүзҡ„дёҖжӢјпјҢиҝҳжҜ” claude еҝ«гҖӮ

з”ҡиҮіж„ҹи§үеҶҷд»Јз ҒиғҪеҠӣйғҪиғҪз•Ҙи¶…иұҶеҢ…иҖҒеёҲдёҖдёўдёў ...

зҪ—иҖҒеёҲзңҹжҳҜ YYDS пјҢиў«еңҲзІүдәҶ ...
е°Ҹзұіmimo жңҖж–°жЁЎеһӢ е’Ң minimax 2.7 high-speed йғҪдёҚй”ҷ .

еҶ·зҹҘиҜҶпјҡ

е…¶е®һзӣӣеӨ§еҲӣж–°йҷўжҳҜжңҖж—©жҸҗеҮәжқҘ AIеҠ©жүӢзҡ„ жҲ‘и®°еҫ—еңЁ4е№ҙеүҚе°ұеңЁжҸҗиҝҷдёӘпјҢз„¶еҗҺжҸҗеҮәжқҘ Agent зҡ„еҮ еӨ§иҰҒзҙ пјҲ д»Җд№ҲеҲҶеұӮи®°еҝҶ гҖҒдё»еҠЁжҖ§ гҖҒ  дёҖиҮҙжҖ§ гҖҒ и¶Ҡз”Ёи¶ҠжҮӮдҪ  зӯүзӯү пјү гҖӮ

4 - 5 е№ҙеүҚ пјҢ йӮЈж—¶еҖҷ openai еҲҡеҮәжқҘпјҲ е…¶е®һ openaiйғҪжІЎеҮәжқҘзҡ„ж—¶еҖҷзӣӣеӨ§еҲӣж–°йҷўе°ұеңЁз ”з©¶ йў„и®ӯз»ғжҷәиғҪдҪ“ ... пјүпјҢ

еҫҲи¶…еүҚең°жҸҗеҲ°

еҪ“ж—¶ еҗҰжҺү perplexity зҡ„ж—¶еҖҷпјҢ йӮЈж—¶еҖҷ perplexity зҡ„дј°еҖјиҝҳжҳҜзҺ°еңЁзҡ„ 1/10 дёҚеҲ° ...

иҷҪз„¶дј°еҖјиҠӮиҠӮй«ҳж”ҖпјҢ иҷҪз„¶еҗҺйқў perplexity жӢҝеҲ°дәҶиҪҜй“¶зҡ„жҠ•иө„ ...

еҪ“ж—¶е°ұи§үеҫ—пјҢж—Ҙжң¬зҡ„еӯҷиҖҒеӨҙ жҳҜдәәеӮ»й’ұеӨҡпјҢ иў«wework pianеҫ—иҝҳдёҚеӨҹеҗёеҸ–ж•ҷи®ӯ ...

ж—©жңҹ зҡ„ж—¶еҖҷ пјҢ еҫҲеӨҡдәәд»Ҙдёә жҷәиғҪдҪ“ == chatbot пјҢйҷҲиҖҒжқҝд№ҹжҳҜиҜҙ пјҢдёҚеҜ№пјҢ дёҚжҳҜиҝҷж ·еӯҗ гҖӮзӣҙжҺҘжӢ”й«ҳдәҶжүҖжңүдәәзҡ„и®ӨзҹҘ ...

дҪҶжҳҜ пјҢи®ӨзҹҘй«ҳд№ҹдёҚйЎ¶з”Ё пјҢжІЎжү§иЎҢеҠӣ ...

еҘҮжҖӘпјҢжңҖиҝ‘еӨ§еҺӮеҸ‘зҡ„еІ—дҪҚе’ҢжұӮиҒҢзҡ„дәәйғҪе°‘дәҶгҖӮ

жҳҜеӨ§е®¶йғҪжңүж»Ўж„Ҹзҡ„е·ҘдҪңдәҶеҗ—пјҹ
